channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Portland Trail Blazers
channel.description: Portland Trail Blazers trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: I've been saying for over a week that I believe Steve …
item.description: Jake Madison: While I don't have a specific date or timeline my understanding is we are close to the end of the Pelicans head coaching search. I've been saying for over a week that I believe Steve Hetzel is the choice and still think that. Jamahl Mosley also remains a possibility

item.title: Krejčí would certainly not have been a "needle-mover" …
item.description: Krejčí would certainly not have been a "needle-mover" – he was not a playoff rotation player for Portland – but he would have represented a new lever Nurse could pull. He would have given the Sixers a bit more optionality on the wing and some much-needed three-point shooting. But the Sixers misread Krejčí's market and, in turn, mistimed their efforts to acquire him, the source said. When the deadline came and went without the Sixers adding a single player to their roster, that relatively min

item.title: “I’ve known him for many, many years,” Ujiri said of …
item.description: Masai Ujiri made his first big move last week, when the Mavericks hired Mike Schmitz as general manager. Schmitz got his start working for the independent scouting service DraftExpress. He was an ESPN analyst for five years and then became the Portland Trail Blazers assistant general manager in 2022. Although Portland’s draft success was mixed in Schmitz’s four years there, he helped drive the decision to trade for Deni Avdija, who was named an All-Star this past season. “I’ve known him for many

item.title: Stotts just finished his second year as Kerr's lead …
item.description: Stotts just finished his second year as Kerr's lead assistant and offensive coordinator. His nearly three-decade coaching career includes a nine-season run as head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers. Stotts informed Kerr late in the regular season that he didn't intend to return and told ESPN he is leaving the Warriors on good terms and remains open to NBA head coaching opportunities. "I enjoyed my time with Steve, the staff and players," Stotts said. "My two years there were fulfilling. Nothin

item.title: Anonymous NBA scout on Holiday’s future in Portland: …
item.description: Anonymous NBA scout on Holiday’s future in Portland: “You’re locked in. That’s a lot of money. I don’t think he’s valued at that number anymore. And you’ve got two more years of it. Obviously he got overpaid because (Boston) won the championship and he was valued high. But it’s not like he performed like a $35 million player. I like him. I just think, ‘How are you going to use him moving forward? Dame (Lillard) is coming back and you have Scoot.’ I will say, Jrue’s best value right now, if I was

item.title: Season: “Jrue is just the ultimate glue guy, a true …
item.description: Anonymous NBA scout on Holiday’s season: “Jrue is just the ultimate glue guy, a true professional on both sides of the floor. When they traded for him, it was like, “Why are they doing this? Let him go somewhere he can compete for a championship.” But Jrue is going to play hard every night, do what’s needed to be done to win games, whether that’s play on the ball, off the ball, whatever. And he was good for them. I love Jrue and the way he complements Toumani (Camara) defensively. That’s two goo

item.title: Jrue Holiday hopes to remain in Portland
item.description: After the Trail Blazers were eliminated from the playoffs by San Antonio, guard Jrue Holiday made it clear he wants to back in Portland again for 2026/27, according to Joe Freeman of The Oregonian. “I hope so. I don’t like being traded and moved and stuff,” Holiday said. “I like being a part of something and building with the team and seeing the progress and going through the progress with them. So I hope so. I’d love to be here. “Honestly, just kind of how we ended the season, fighting and claw

item.title: Matisse Thybulle addresses free agency
item.description: Matisse Thybulle on his looming free agency: “I don’t have expectations. I don’t really know how the whole proper free agency thing goes. I was a restricted free agent before, but this is very different. Expectations? Don’t have any, don’t know what to expect. But I would love to be a part of this team again. Like I said, great group with a lot of potential. Good people. I talk about this with, like, my friends and even the older players on the team who’ve been traded; I don’t know if everyone o

item.title: “They’ve all helped me in different ways and all their …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Donte DiVincenzo says he’s spoken to Jayson Tatum, Tyrese Haliburton and Damian Lillard about the process of coming back from an Achilles. “They’ve all helped me in different ways and all their journeys are different.”

item.title: Tom Dundon defends hotel room decision: 'If that's too hard for people, then I'm not right for them'
item.description: Blazers Lead: Tom Dundon explained the hotel room story in Phoenix Says that since the Play-In game was scheduled so late, the hotel they were staying at wanted them to pay for an extra night and did not offer late checkout So they did that for the coaches and players, but for the staff, including Tom, since a ballroom downstairs was already reserved for lunch at 1:45, they did not pay for an extra room and instead had them hang out in the ballroom “If that’s too hard for people, then I’m not ri

item.title: "I made a mistake. I just don’t understand the …
item.description: Sean Highkin: In this interview Tom Dundon directly contradicts Joe Cronin taking the blame at exit interviews for not traveling the two-ways. I never believed for a second it was Joe's call but good to hear that from the boss directly: "I made a mistake. I just don’t understand the league."

item.title: “I’m not playing him just because he gets views. If he …
item.description: Blazers Lead: Rich Paul asked Tom Dundon: How do you maximize the viewership that Yang Hansen brings? Dundon acknowledged how the views peaked early on and then tapered off because Hansen didn’t play, to which Paul said “you gotta play him” Dundon said: “I’m not doing that” “I’m not playing him just because he gets views. If he earns it, he’ll play, of course, I’m not stupid. It would be great if he was good, and I hope he will be good…it’s early” Notably, he added, “the guys in Portland are ver

item.title: Terry Stotts interested in returning to Blazers
item.description: Terry Stotts has notified the Golden State Warriors he will. not be back as Steve Kerr’s No. 1 assistant coach next season. That opens the door to some speculation. Would he be interested in returning to the Trail Blazers as the head coach? “I would love to come back to the Blazers and Portland,” he told me via text Friday.

item.title: "I'm just not gonna waste $100 million just because …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Tom Dundon said it was a "mistake" to not send 2-way players on the road. He said Blazers spend $100 million more than the Hurricanes. From his interview on the Game Over pod: "I'm just not gonna waste $100 million just because someone wants to write an article calling me cheap"

item.title: Tom Dundon on spending to build a roster: I want to win more than I want to make money
item.description: On whether he’s willing to enter the tax to build a winning roster: Tom Dundon: “Joe (Cronin) called me — and I don’t even know if it was allowed — he called me at the trade deadline. Technically we didn’t own the team yet, (but) he had a deal — actually two different deals — but one in particular where we would have gone into the tax. And it would have been whatever; $20 million. I was fine with it. I want to run the business properly. But I want to win more than I want to make money. And that

item.title: “I just don’t want to waste money. I want to invest …
item.description: On the notion he’s cheap: Tom Dundon: “My hockey team has been over the cap many times. They were the lowest in the league. They were at the floor when I bought them. You can go over the cap in hockey. If you have a hurt player, you can replace them. So I’ve been over the cap multiple times because there was a rule that allowed you to go over the cap. And at the same time, when I walk out of a room, I turn off the lights. And so that my friends make fun of me all the time. They’re telling me how

item.title: Tom Dundon on keeping Blazers in Portland: If the funding bill passes, this is a non-story
item.description: On the subject of moving the Blazers out of Portland: Tom Dundon: “When I bought the Hurricanes, all I heard — because I was from Texas — we were going to move the team to Houston. Moving a team is difficult. We didn’t move the Hurricanes. We ended up getting a deal done. We went through the same thing in Portland. Before I even bought the team, I had an agreement with the city and the state. We had an agreement in principle. They’ve already approved half of it. Assuming that all gets done, then

item.title: “We would deal with that if it ever comes up. But, …
item.description: On what happens if the funding bill falls apart: Tom Dundon: “We would deal with that if it ever comes up. But, obviously, we have to solve problems when we have problems. Today, the problem we’re trying to solve is just to get a lease extension and get the building renovated. And those are fun things to do, right? Making the building better and adding some energy to that building. Those are things I enjoy doing.”

item.title: Blazers interested in Jared Dudley, Micah Nori, Steve Hetzel as head coach
item.description: League sources tell The Stein Line that the Blazers, meanwhile, have expressed interest in Minnesota assistant coach Micah Nori in addition to previously reported candidates Jared Dudley (Denver) and Hetzel … while Portland-based Sean Highkin reported recently that Lakers assistant coach Greg St. Jean is also on Portland's list

item.title: Bulls, Magic and Trail Blazers are all slated to make …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Bulls, Magic and Trail Blazers are all slated to make formal interview requests for all three franchises’ respective coaching vacancies this week, sources say.

item.title: I've also been advised that Chicago is likely to …
item.description: I've also been advised that Chicago is likely to formally request to interview Tiago Splitter, who is still under contract with Portland for next season even as the Trail Blazers actively speak to external candidates about their coaching job. Splitter went 42-39 as Portland's interim coach this season and steered the team to its first playoff berth in five years after Chauncey Billups was placed on indefinite leave due to a federal gambling probe just one game into the season.

item.title: Was told around 70 people were let go today in the …
item.description: Sean Highkin: Was told around 70 people were let go today in the Blazers' layoffs on the business side. Know some of the names but out of respect to them I'll let them announce it when and how they will.

item.title: Talked to one person who survived the cuts today who …
item.description: Sean Highkin: Talked to one person who survived the cuts today who said it feels like they just looked at a spreadsheet of salaries and cut the highest ones without any regard for what anyone does and how important they are.

item.title: I have indeed been let go after 18+ years with the …
item.description: Casey Holdahl: I have indeed been let go after 18+ years with the @trailblazers. My sincerest thanks to all of you who have read/listened/watched/engaged with my work over the years.

item.title: "Today, as part of our plans to position the …
item.description: Joe Freeman: From Blazers Pres. Dewayne Hankins: "Today, as part of our plans to position the organization for the future, we made the difficult decision to restructure several areas of the business. These changes impacted talented people who have helped shape the Trail Blazers ... (cont)"

item.title: We are deeply grateful for their contributions, their …
item.description: Joe Freeman: "...over many years. We are deeply grateful for their contributions, their leadership and the care they showed every day for our team, our fans and the Portland community. (cont)"

item.title: Our focus now is supporting those affected through the …
item.description: Joe Freeman: "... Our focus now is supporting those affected through the transition and positioning the organization for long-term success."

item.title: Virtually the entire pr staff has been broomed out. …
item.description: Chris Mannix: Virtually the entire pr staff has been broomed out. Roughly 75 people fired today.

item.title: The employees were told they had been laid off and …
item.description: Employees learned about the layoffs during a Tuesday morning group video call, which lasted roughly two minutes. The employees were told they had been laid off and would receive more information at their personal email accounts. The layoffs affected both business and basketball operations and ranged from senior vice presidents to public relations employees to content producers.

item.title: One high-ranking team official told The …
item.description: One high-ranking team official told The Oregonian/OregonLive that the Blazers were one of the largest organizations in the NBA, an unsustainable reality considering they fetch the league’s lowest sponsorship revenue and rank near the bottom in ticket revenue.

item.title: The majority of the cuts, but not all, were in …
item.description: Before Tuesday, the Blazers had roughly 215 employees on the business side, and about 390 employees in total. The majority of the cuts, but not all, were in business, a source said.

item.title: Mostly lower- and mid-level staffers. Joe Cronin and …
item.description: Sean Highkin: Blazers cuts are mostly business side but there were a few in basketball ops, I'm told. Mostly lower- and mid-level staffers. Joe Cronin and his three top assistants (Andrae Patterson, Sergi Oliva and Asjha Jones) are all still with the organization.

item.title: Blazers laying off 20 percent of their staff
item.description: Brandon Mullen: The Portland Trail Blazers are laying off 20% of their staff, according to a source with knowledge of the decision. Staff were told that “the decision came at ownership’s direction."

item.title: Giannis has interest in a few places. I was told, I …
item.description: Darren Wolfson: "Let's not forget, Phil, Giannis has the hammer because he's essentially in a contract year. There's that player option, but wherever he goes, he's signing an extension. I could certainly concoct a great package that the Portland Trail Blazers could put together for Giannis. But if Giannis through his representation signals to the Blazers, I'm not signing a contract extension, you're not giving up Scoot Henderson and a million draft picks. You're just not doing that. The word is,

item.title: ESPN today announced it has signed senior NBA writer …
item.description: ESPN today announced it has signed senior NBA writer Ben Golliver. He will cover the league with national analysis, reporting and feature storytelling. Golliver joins ESPN after covering the NBA nationally since 2010, most recently for The Washington Post. His previous stops also include Sports Illustrated and CBSSports.com, and he began his career covering the Portland Trail Blazers in 2007. Based in Los Angeles, Golliver will contribute across ESPN.com and other ESPN platforms.

item.title: Blazers scout and WNBA legend and Hall of Famer Tina …
item.description: Sean Highkin: Blazers scout and WNBA legend and Hall of Famer Tina Thompson announced on Instagram that she was one of the cuts.

item.title: But when I look at the Trail Blazers roster, that …
item.description: Draymond Green: "Jason Kidd from Oakland. Damian Lillard also from Oakland. Point guard. You look at Scoot Henderson, a young guard. How can Jason Kidd help him? So when you take a step back and you look at those jobs, I definitely can see Jason Kidd taking the Trail Blazers job. Now, however, it's been widely known that the owner of their team doesn't want to pay the head coach. It's been widely publicized. A lot of people have been talking about it. And quite honestly, you're making $10 millio

item.title: Rahsaan Gethers, VP of Basketball Communications with …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: Rahsaan Gethers, VP of Basketball Communications with the Portland Trail Blazers, was part of the mass layoffs made by the team yesterday. Very talented PR guru who was respected by the players and coaches alike and also had a productive working relationship with the media.

item.title: Micah Nori among finalists for Blazers head coaching job
item.description: Marc Stein: Minnesota's Micah Nori is also a finalist for the Portland coaching vacancy, @TheSteinLine has learned. Nori, Blazers interim coach Tiago Splitter and LA Clippers assistant coach Jeff Van Gundy are the known finalists.

item.title: Tiago Splitter still an option for Blazers head coach position
item.description: Marc Stein: Blazers interim coach Tiago Splitter is also a finalist for the full-time post, sources tell @TheSteinLine. The other known finalist per @ChrisBHaynes: Jeff Van Gundy. @JakeLFischer reported earlier this week that Splitter, amid Chicago interest, was back in the Portland race.

item.title: Portland Trail Blazers to interview Jeff Van Gundy for head coach opening
item.description: Chris Haynes: Portland Trail Blazers have requested permission to meet with Los Angeles Clippers assistant Jeff Van Gundy as he has emerged as one of the finalists for their head coach opening, league sources tell me.

item.title: Jerry Stackhouse interviewing for Blazers head coaching job this weekend
item.description: Jake Fischer: In addition to his interest from Chicago, I’m told Jerry Stackhouse will also be interviewing for Portland’s next head coaching position this weekend.

item.title: Blazers add Tyler Lashbrook and Mike Williams to advanced stage of head coaching search
item.description: League sources tell The Stein Line contributor Jake Fischer, furthermore, that two additional previously unreported names have also advanced to those stages: Boston's Tyler Lashbrook and Utah's Mike Williams. Sources say various candidates will fly into Portland this weekend as the process continues. Portland's search has been multilayered to date and thus challenging even for NBA coaching insiders to track, given that some candidates have spoken primarily to Blazers general manager Joe Cronin,

item.title: Blazers have come away impressed by Utah Jazz …
item.description: Chris Haynes: Portland Trail Blazers have come away impressed by Utah Jazz assistant Mike Williams and he is among the final pool of candidates in consideration for the head coach opening, league sources tell me.

item.title: Donovan Clingan not happy with the All-Defense team …
item.description: Sean Highkin: Donovan Clingan not happy with the All-Defense team selections, but not on his own behalf

item.title: Blazers interim head coach Tiago Splitter has remained …
item.description: Trail Blazers interim head coach Tiago Splitter has remained in contact with Portland throughout the team’s head coaching search and is expected to be among Portland’s finalists for the job, league sources told HoopsHype. Meanwhile, the Chicago Bulls also have interest in Splitter for their head coaching vacancy.

item.title: Blazers interviewed Hawks assistant coach Bryan Bailey
item.description: Quietly, the Trail Blazers also interviewed Hawks assistant coach Bryan Bailey for Portland’s head coaching position, league sources told HoopsHype. Bailey has been an assistant coach for Quin Snyder with the Hawks and the Utah Jazz. He was also an assistant coach for Jazz coach Will Hardy.

item.title: Portland also called the Cleveland Cavaliers for …
item.description: Portland also called the Cleveland Cavaliers for permission to speak with assistant coach Omar Cook early in the process, which was believed to be for a possible assistant coaching position, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Thus, while the stars may not align on paper for a …
item.description: It’s worth noting that former Dallas Mavericks head coach Jason Kidd has quietly been connected to Portland within league circles. Yet, will that chatter lead to anything more substantive now or in the future? Now, Portland’s current price range for a head coach is well below Kidd’s remaining contract with the Mavericks, which is for four years and $40 million, according to ESPN. Kidd also expressed a desire to make front office personnel decisions before Dallas hired Masai Ujiri and Portland g

item.title: The Utah Jazz will name Shane Fenske as general …
item.description: The Utah Jazz will name Shane Fenske as general manager of their G League affiliate, the Salt Lake City Stars, league sources told HoopsHype. Fenske is also Utah’s assistant general manager. Previously, the Yale graduate worked for the Boston Celtics and Portland Trail Blazers.

item.title: Patrick St. Andrews joins Bucks as an assistant coach
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks will add Patrick St. Andrews as an assistant coach, reuniting him with coach Taylor Jenkins. He was an assistant coach for the Blazers. Prior, he was an assistant coach with Jenkins on the Grizzlies and with Mike Budenholzer on the Bucks and Hawks.

item.title: Patrick St. Andrews was one of Tiago Splitter’s most …
item.description: Sean Highkin: Patrick St. Andrews was one of Tiago Splitter’s most important assistants this year and was responsible for managing rotations and minutes restrictions when half the team was coming back from injury.

item.title: Robert Williams picking up buzz as a free agent
item.description: Now, Wemby's a freak, I get it. He's a once-in-a-lifetime sort of talent. But, is there anybody out there that the Lakers could sign and go, "At least now you've got a competitive big man out there." Bobby Marks: "The one name that picked up the most buzz, a lot of teams in Chicago last week, was Robert Williams. That's the name. You have to be cautious with that because of his games availability, the minutes. That's the one guy, I don't want to say neutralized Victor Wembanyama, but I think gua

item.title: Hawks, Rockets and Blazers interested in Jaylen Brown
item.description: There is a belief in some corners of the league that Atlanta, Houston and Portland all have legitimate trade interest in Jaylen Brown. Which is something to file away if you're looking for participants that might be interested in joining an eventual multi-team Antetokounmpo trade construction.

item.title: Tom Dundon on laying off 70+ Blazers employees: There were too many people, less layers gives more accountability
item.description: The one thing that is directly impactful was the reduction of staff, 70-plus people, last week. Why were such extensive layoffs necessary in your mind? Tom Dundon: They have twice as many people as we have in Carolina, and my experience is that less layers gives people more accountability and they do a better job. Then you know that you’re getting a great job done and you don’t end up in the situation where many companies end up, which is everyone’s looking around for somebody else to do it, com

item.title: The world tends to want to highlight or look for …
item.description: How do you reconcile the success you’ve had in Raleigh, and the way people feel about your tenure there, with the way you’re viewed in Portland? Having proof of concept in one place but people are still highly skeptical in the other? Tom Dundon: The world tends to want to highlight or look for problems. I don’t know if that’s more pronounced in Portland or not. Clearly, I’m doing stuff and people have that perception of me. It would be crazy if I didn’t think about what my role in it is. But it

item.title: It seems like Portland’s more political. It’s more …
item.description: But it does seem like it’s more challenging. At least in Raleigh, it’s felt like maybe more of the essential people were rowing in the same direction than it feels in Portland. Tom Dundon: It seems like Portland’s more political. It’s more emotional. It’s a more emotional subject. North Carolina has a fund to bring sporting events to the state. There’s a lot of businesses moving in, they’re lowering their income tax. They’re doing a lot of things. They’re in a different place in terms of economi

item.title: Donte DiVincenzo to return during the 2026-27 season?
item.description: It is far too early in the recovery process to predict when Donte DiVincenzo will return to action. Tearing an Achilles tendon is among the most serious injuries in the game, often keeping players out for a year or more. Boston’s Jayson Tatum returned in less than 10 months, but that is considered an outlier. Still, DiVincenzo is trying to follow Tatum’s lead. He flew to New York for surgery less than 24 hours after suffering the injury, following a similar timeline that Tatum went through. DiVi

item.title: Yes I would
item.description: Damian Lillard: Yes I would.
